data.content_ref: inline:The mechanism relies on 'coordinated external pressure from maritime raiders' overwhelming local militaries and trade networks, yet the scope excludes Egypt solely because it 'repelled the Sea Peoples.' This creates an explanatory asymmetry: if the Sea Peoples were a force capable of simultaneously collapsing Mycenaean Greece, the Hittite Empire, Ugarit, and the Levantine city-states, the account must explain why Egypt—geographically and economically integrated into the same Eastern Mediterranean system—was able to repel them while all others failed. Merely excluding Egypt from the scope does not address why the same external pressure produced catastrophic collapse elsewhere but only 'diminished' survival in Egypt, leaving the mechanism's claimed universality unjustified.
